name: Mise en Play: Play as a Creative Tool
description:
Every day, without thinking about it, you make sense of your surroundings through what you eat, what you hear, what your body tells you. We are all meaning-making creatures, and our earliest instinct was to do this through play. Play is a creative act in itself. Growing up, play was how we explored, expressed, and understood the world. Somewhere along the way, we stopped. Here is an invitation to start again.

In this edition of Mise En Play, we use the same lens to explore different exhibits as a part of CALORIE. A space where many different perspectives and processes come together, and in doing so, we begin to see creativity not as a skill but as a practice. That practice is what we invite you into.

Expect hands-on activities that engage all your senses. You need no background in art or design, only an openness to notice, engage, and explore. Leave with a new way of observing your environment and documenting your experiences.

Join experimentors Nirupama and Shreya for a programme that treats creativity as something that belongs to everyone.

name: Malleshwaram Bakery Walk
description:
What can a bakery reveal about a city?

Join us for a guided walk through some of Bengaluru’s neighbourhood bakeries as we trace the histories, communities, and everyday food cultures that shaped them. Beginning in the heart of Malleshwaram, the walk moves through bakeries known for warm khara buns, sweet dilpasands, plain bread cake and more.

name: Nature and Nurture: How The Environment Shapes Our Genes
description:
How does our environment and genetics affect our health?

The nature versus nurture debate has evolved into a more integrated view, recognising that genes and environment work together throughout our lives. While our genetic makeup – nature – provides the blueprint for potential traits, the environment – nurture – shapes how these genes are expressed, often through ‘epigenetic’ mechanisms. 

Join Sanjeev Galande as he explores how understanding human behaviour, health, and development requires considering both genetic and environmental factors. Particularly, highlighting the technological breakthroughs and transdisciplinary approaches that have deepened our understanding of disease susceptibility, with a focus on lifestyle disorders.

name: Gulistan (The Land of Flowers) and Other Histories
description:
How do plants travel across cultures, histories, and oceans?

Join Gopa Trivedi, artist of Gulistan, which maps a garden of ingredients that travelled far before landing on Indian soil—seeds carried by trade, colonial routes, and ocean currents. In this talk, she shares the explorations, thoughts, questions, and journeys that shape and inform her visual arts practice: through images, ideas, and encounters that have accumulated over a decade of working at the intersection of the personal, the historical, and the deeply uncertain.

name: The Language of Materials - Understanding Art Through Science in CALORIE
description:
What happens when we look at artworks through the lens of material science? From palm oil and sugar crystals to plastics and DNA. The materials artists choose, shapes not only how an artwork looks and feels but also how they give life to ideas.

Artists and Scientists ask similar questions about materials:, How do they change? What do they reveal? And what are they capable of becoming? 

Through food and a diverse range of natural, synthetic, and biological materials, we invite you to look closely and explore the language of materials.

Join us as we uncover how the understanding of material science can open new ways of seeing art. Together, we will explore how materials react, transform and communicate – revealing stories, processes and possibilities embedded within them.
